response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically broad, staff-level software engineer with over 15 years of shipped product experience, but the role demands 8+ years of production AI/GenAI work — a bar the resume does not approach. His AI exposure appears to have begun in April 2025 at re:cinq and is described in general terms (MCP code generators, agentic workflows) with no evidence of RAG systems, vector databases, or named multi-agent frameworks. Critically, Python is absent from his skills list despite being a primary required language. His TypeScript depth, AWS CDK usage, and high-throughput data pipeline work at Dunnhumby are legitimate positives, and the MCP server work is a direct signal — but taken together, the AI-specific depth is insufficient for a staff forward-deployed GenAI engineer role where customers expect a domain expert, not a generalist pivoting into AI.
strengths: MCP (Model Context Protocol) server and framework contributions at re:cinq map directly onto a listed key technology, showing current-generation agentic tooling awareness
High-throughput data pipeline experience at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency async I/O) demonstrates the data engineering foundation the role requires
Consistent staff-level and senior IC track record across web, mobile, embedded, and cloud domains with proven TypeScript and AWS CDK usage
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume's skills list, despite being a primary required language for AI system development in this role
Production GenAI experience is effectively limited to the current role started April 2025 — roughly one month — falling far short of the 8-year minimum requirement
No evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation pipelines, or named multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, or comparable), which are core technical requirements
